category_title: Students with a history of school failure and resistance to traditional academic environments
scenario_title: Resistance to academic writing tasks and traditional authority
minimal_context: The student is asked to write a formal summary of electrical safety procedures during a Professional initiation Social Guarantee Programme class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please write a formal summary of the electrical safety procedures we just reviewed.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Why do I gotta write this down? I can just do the wires and stuff. I do not think I am clever enough for this.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to practice your writing. Just write down what you know on the paper.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Pushes the paper away) This ain't useful for nothing. I was forced to go to class anyway.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is a required task for this program. Please pick up your pen and complete the assignment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: You just want to make me look stupid like always. The teachers were against me, and you're doing the exact same thing. I'm not writing anything.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please write a formal summary of the electrical safety procedures we just reviewed.

STUDENT: Why do I gotta write this down? I can just do the wires and stuff. I do not think I am clever enough for this.

TUTOR: You need to practice your writing. Just write down what you know on the paper.

STUDENT: (Pushes the paper away) This ain't useful for nothing. I was forced to go to class anyway.

TUTOR: It is a required task for this program. Please pick up your pen and complete the assignment.

STUDENT: You just want to make me look stupid like always. The teachers were against me, and you're doing the exact same thing. I'm not writing anything.

task.content: Academic tasks involving writing, where their language is inexact and lacks precision, heavily dependent on slang, which is then transferred to their writing

context.environment: Professional initiation Social Guarantee Programme groups from the electricity section
context.social_setting: Classroom environment where students are interacting with a teacher they see as an intruder in their lives
context.time_context: The first few months of work

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the teacher enforces traditional school patterns and is perceived as a distant authority figure
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Displays mistrust, scorn, a certain aggressiveness and a defensive attitude towards the teacher; rebellious against rules and authority
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: They see us as people who are distant and who over many years may have made their 'misery' evident; low self-esteem
related_quotes: I was forced to go to class.

behavior_patterns.condition: When faced with academic tasks rather than manipulative tasks
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Very spontaneous, impulsive and not given to reflection; usually did not complete the tasks set by the teacher
behavior_patterns.internal_state: I do not think I am clever enough
related_quotes: I do not think I am clever enough.
